info.name: ai-expense-tracker API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the ai-expense-tracker backend.

**Setup:**
1. Run the app on port 8080
2. Call **Register** or **Login** — the token is saved to `{{token}}` automatically
3. All protected endpoints pick up `{{token}}` via the Authorization header

**Collection variables:**
- `base_url` — `http://localhost:8080`
- `token` — populated automatically by the Login request

item.name: Auth
item.description: Registration and login. The Login request automatically saves the JWT to the `{{token}}` collection variable.

request.description:
Creates a new user account and returns a JWT access token. The token is identical to what `/api/auth/login` returns, so you can use this to start immediately without a separate login call.

Fields:
- `name` — display name
- `email` — used as the login username
- `password` — plain text (hashed with BCrypt server-side)

Returns `accessToken`, `tokenType`, and `expiresInSeconds`.

Authenticates an existing user and returns a JWT access token.

The post-response script automatically saves `accessToken` from the response body into the `{{token}}` collection variable. All subsequent requests in this collection use that token via `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`.

Token TTL is 600 seconds (10 minutes) by default.

exec: console.warn('No accessToken in response — check credentials');

item.name: Transactions
item.description:
CRUD operations for financial transactions. All requests require a valid JWT.

Transaction types:
- `EXPENSE` — money out of an account
- `INCOME` — money into an account
- `TRANSFER` — money moved between two accounts (requires `toAccountId`)

Amount is always sent as a positive number; the backend stores it as negative for debits.

Payment mode IDs (from seed data):
- 1 = Credit Card (LIABILITY)
- 2 = Debit Card (ASSET)
- 3 = Net Banking (ASSET)
- 4 = UPI (ASSET)
- 5 = Cash (ASSET)
- 6 = Digital Wallet (ASSET)

Category IDs (from seed data):
- 1 = Groceries, 2 = Dining Out, 3 = Rent/EMI, 4 = Utilities, 5 = Fuel/Transportation
- 6 = Health & Medical, 7 = Insurance, 8 = Shopping, 9 = Entertainment & OTT
- 10 = Education, 11 = Investments, 12 = Gifts & Donations, 13 = Travel & Vacation
- 14 = Maintenance & Repairs, 15 = Miscellaneous

body.raw:
{
  "transactionId": null,
  "type": "EXPENSE",
  "description": "Grocery shopping at DMart",
  "amount": 850.00,
  "transactionDate": "20-05-2026",
  "paymentModeId": 4,
  "accountId": 1,
  "categoryId": 1,
  "toAccountId": null,
  "transferId": null
}

request.description:
Records an expense transaction. The account balance is reduced by the amount.

- `type`: `EXPENSE`
- `paymentModeId: 4` = UPI (ASSET type — balance must be sufficient)
- `categoryId: 1` = Groceries
- `accountId`: the ID of the account to debit (check your DB for the actual ID created by CmdRunner)
- `transactionDate` format: `dd-MM-yyyy`

Records an income transaction. The account balance is increased by the amount.

- `type`: `INCOME`
- `paymentModeId: 3` = Net Banking (ASSET)
- `categoryId: 15` = Miscellaneous (use appropriate category for salary)

Moves money between two accounts owned by the same user. Creates two linked transaction records (a debit on `accountId` and a credit on `toAccountId`) sharing a `transferId` UUID.

- `type`: `TRANSFER`
- `accountId`: source account (debited)
- `toAccountId`: destination account (credited)
- `paymentModeId: 3` = Net Banking

Returns all transactions belonging to the authenticated user, ordered by creation time descending.

Each transaction includes: `transactionId`, `type`, `description`, `amount`, `transactionDate`, linked category, account, and payment mode.

Deletes a transaction by ID. The request is scoped to the authenticated user — users cannot delete transactions belonging to other users.

Replace `1` in the URL with the actual transaction ID from the **Get All Transactions** response.

Note: this does **not** reverse the account balance. Use the Update (PATCH) endpoint to do a balance-neutral correction first if needed.

item.name: AI Input
item.description:
Natural language transaction parsing powered by Google Gemini (gemini-2.0-flash).

**Async flow** (`POST /api/ai-input`):
1. Submit raw text → get back a `taskId`
2. Open SSE stream at `GET /api/notifications/status/{taskId}` to receive status updates (PENDING → PROCESSING → COMPLETED)
3. On COMPLETED, the parsed transaction is automatically saved using the user's default account and payment mode from `UserConfig`

**Sync flow** (`POST /api/ai-input/sync`):
Submit raw text → get back a `TransactionRequestDto` immediately. The client then fills in `accountId` and `paymentModeId` and POSTs to `/api/transactions`.

Supported languages: English and Hindi.

request.description:
Submits a free-form text description of a transaction for async AI parsing.

Returns a `taskId` and a confirmation message. The backend queues the task; the scheduler picks it up within ~5 seconds, calls Gemini to parse it, and fires an SSE notification when done.

Once COMPLETED, the transaction is auto-saved to the DB using the user's default account and payment mode (set in `UserConfig`).

Example inputs:
- `"Spent 450 rupees on petrol today"`
- `"Received 5000 from freelance work yesterday"`
- `"Aaj 200 rupaye grocery pe kharch kiye"` (Hindi)

Synchronously parses a natural language transaction description using Gemini and returns a `TransactionRequestDto` directly — no queuing, no SSE.

The response has `type`, `description`, `amount`, `transactionDate`, and `categoryId` populated from the AI. `accountId` and `paymentModeId` will be `null` — the client must supply them before POSTing to `/api/transactions`.

Use this when you want to show the user the parsed result for confirmation/editing before saving.

Opens a Server-Sent Events stream for a specific async AI task. Receives status updates as the task progresses through PENDING → PROCESSING → COMPLETED (or FAILED).

`{{task_id}}` is saved automatically when you run **Submit NL Input (Async)**.

Note: Postman has limited SSE support — use a browser or a dedicated SSE client (e.g. `curl -N`) for best results:
```
curl -N -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' http://localhost:8080/api/notifications/status/<taskId>
```

item.name: Insights
item.description:
AI-generated spending insights powered by Google Gemini.

Insights are generated automatically by a background scheduler:
- **WEEKLY** — every Monday at 09:00
- **MONTHLY** — 1st of every month at 09:00

Each insight analyses the user's transactions for the relevant lookback window (7 or 30 days) and produces a plain-text summary covering: total income vs expenses, top spending categories, notable patterns, and actionable tips.

request.description:
Returns all WEEKLY insights for the authenticated user, ordered most-recent first.

Each insight has: `id`, `type`, `insightText`, `status`, `createdAt` (epoch ms).

Insights are generated every Monday at 09:00 by the scheduler. If none exist yet, the response is an empty array.

Returns all MONTHLY insights for the authenticated user, ordered most-recent first.

Insights are generated on the 1st of every month at 09:00 by the scheduler, covering the previous 30 days of transactions.
Returns all insights (both WEEKLY and MONTHLY) for the authenticated user, ordered most-recent first. Omit the `type` query parameter to get everything.

Returns only the single most recent WEEKLY insight for the authenticated user.

Responds with `204 No Content` if no weekly insight has been generated yet.

Returns only the single most recent MONTHLY insight for the authenticated user.

Responds with `204 No Content` if no monthly insight has been generated yet.
